title: Inhalers and airflow adaptors therefor
abstract: The present disclosure describes an inhaler for the inhalation of powder medication. The inhaler has a body and at least one reservoir containing powder medication, the body having an air inlet and an outlet for the transmission to a patient of air entering the body through the air inlet and powder medication. The outlet has a total cross-sectional area for flow which is more than 80% of the total cross-sectional area of the air inlet.
claims:
Claims 1 . An inhaler for the inhalation of powder medication, the inhaler having a body and at least one reservoir containing powder medication, the body having an air inlet and an outlet for the transmission to a patient of air entering the body through the air inlet and powder medication, the outlet having a total cross-sectional area for flow which is more than 80% of the total cross-sectional area of the air inlet. An inhaler as claimed in Claim 1 in which the total cross-sectional area of the outlet is more than 85% of the total cross-sectional area of the inlet; optionally less than 125%, for example 90 to 100%, about 95% being one example. An inhaler as claimed in Claim 1 in which the outlet comprises a primary outlet for the transmission of dry powder medicament entrained in air as well as at least one secondary outlet for the transmission of a bypass flow of air alone without medicament. An inhaler as claimed in Claim 3 in which the primary outlet is substantially circular. An inhaler as claimed in Claim 3 or Claim 4 in which the primary outlet has ; cross-sectional areas of 30 to 50 mm 2 , typically 35 to 45 mm 2 , about 38.50 mm being one example. An inhaler as claimed in Claim 3 or Claim 4 or Claim 5 in which the total cross sectional area of all said secondary outlets is 5 to 15 mm 2 , typically 8 to 12 mm 2 about 10.00 mm 2 being one example. 7. An inhaler as claimed in Claim 1 or Claim 2 or Claim 3 in which the total cross- sectional area of the inlet is 40 to 60 mm 2 , typically 45 to 55 mm 2 , about 50.80 mm 2 being one example. 8. An inhaler as claimed in any preceding claim in which the air inlet is generally elliptical in shape and has at least an upper or lower edge portion thereof which is elliptical; the air inlet preferably including at least one grille member extending thereacross parallel to a major axis of the generally elliptical shape of the air inlet. An inhaler as claimed in any preceding claim which includes a deagglomerator which has a swirl chamber which includes at least one inlet port for air without powder medicament, as well as a medicament inlet for air with powder medicament, the area of flow through the at least one inlet port combined with the medicament inlet having a combined cross-sectional area which is less than the total cross-sectional area of the inlet to the body; preferably (a) also less than the cross-sectional area of the outlet and/or (b) about 40 mm 2 . An inhaler as claimed in Claim 9 which is dependent upon Claim 3 in which the combined cross-sectional area is 3 to 5 times larger than the total cross-sectional area of all said secondary outlets, for example about 4 times larger. An inhaler as claimed in any preceding claim in which the outlet is formed at a mouthpiece of the inhaler. An inhaler as claimed in any preceding claim in which a cone angle of a plume of substance emitted by the inhaler is less than 35 degrees. An inhaler as claimed in any preceding claim in which the cross sectional area at a distance 3 cm away from inhaler exit of a plume of substance emitted from the inhaler is less than 6 cm 2 , optionally less than 5 cm 2 , for example about 4.5 cm 2 . An inhaler as claimed in any preceding claim in which at a distance 3 cm away from inhaler exit the ratio of maximum to minimum cross-dimensions of a plume of substance emitted from the inhaler is less than 1 .8, optionally less than 1 .7 or less than 1 .6, 1 .55 being one example. An inhaler as claimed in any preceding claim which includes dry powder medicament. An inhaler as claimed in claim 3 or any preceding claim when dependent upon claim 3, in which the primary and secondary ports are configured such that delivery of Stage 4 particles in a Copley Scientific Next Generation Impactor test at pressure drops of 2 kPa, 4 kPa and 6 kPa varies between a most weight of particles and least weight of particles and the most weight of particles is less than 

 50% more than the least weight, optionally less than 35% more or less than 30% more, about 25% more being one example. An inhaler as claimed in claim 3 or any preceding claim when dependent upon claim 3, in which the primary and secondary ports are configured such that delivery of Stage 5 particles in a Copley Scientific Next Generation Impactor test at pressure drops of 2 kPa, 4 kPa and 6 kPa varies between a most weight of particles and least weight of particles and the most weight of particles is less than 20% more than the least weight, optionally less than 17.5% more or less than 15% more, about 14% more being one example. An inhaler for the inhalation of powder medication, the inhaler having a body and at least one reservoir containing powder medication, the body having an air inlet and an outlet for the transmission to a patient of air entering the body through the air inlet and powder medication, the outlet comprising a primary outlet for the transmission of dry powder medicament entrained in air as well as at least one secondary outlet for the transmission of a bypass flow of air, the primary and secondary outlets being configured to produce a plume of particulate substance emitted by the inhaler with a cone angle of the plume which is less than 35 degrees. An inhaler for the inhalation of powder medication, the inhaler having a body and at least one reservoir containing powder medication, the body having an air inlet and an outlet for the transmission to a patient of air entering the body through the air inlet and powder medication, the outlet comprising a primary outlet for the transmission of dry powder medicament entrained in air as well as at least one secondary outlet for the transmission of a bypass flow of air, the primary and secondary outlets being configured to produce a plume of particulate substance emitted by the inhaler which at a plane 3 cm away from inhaler exit has a cross sectional area which is less than 6 cm 2 . An inhaler for the inhalation of powder medication, the inhaler having a body and at least one reservoir containing powder medication, the body having an air inlet and an outlet for the transmission to a patient of air entering the body through the air inlet and powder medication, the outlet comprising a primary outlet for the transmission of dry powder medicament entrained in air as well as at least one secondary outlet for the transmission of a bypass flow of air, the primary and 

 secondary outlets being configured to produce a plume of particulate substance emitted from the inhaler which at a plane a distance 3 cm from inhaler exit has a ratio of maximum to minimum cross-dimensions which is less than 1.8. 21 . An inhaler for the inhalation of powder medication, the inhaler having a body, at least one reservoir containing powder medication and a swirl chamber for deagglomerating the powder medication, the body having an air inlet and an outlet for the transmission to a patient of air entering the body through the air inlet and powder medication, the outlet comprising a primary outlet for the transmission of dry powder medicament entrained in air as well as at least one secondary outlet for the transmission of a bypass flow of air, the primary and secondary outlets being configured to produce a plume of particulate substance emitted by the inhaler with a plume angle of from about 95 degrees to about 100 degrees. 22. A method of treating a respiratory disease or disorder comprising actuating the inhaler as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 21 to administer a therapeutically effective amount of one or more active ingredients. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the inhaler is a dry powder inhaler and the step of actuating the inhaler comprises inhaling through the inhaler. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein the respiratory disease or disorder is asthma. 25. The method of claim 22, wherein the respiratory disease or disorder is chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. 26. The method of claim 22, wherein the one or more active ingredients includes budesonide and/or formoterol fumarate. 27. The method of claim 22, wherein the one or more active ingredients comprise albuterol or salbutamol sulphate. 28. The method of claim 22, wherein the one or more active ingredients comprise a fluticasone propionate and salmeterol combination. 29. The method of claim 28 in which the salmeterol is salmeterol xinafoate.
